senses.definition: in regard to what someone has said or written

examples.x: According to the weather forecast, it will rain tomorrow.
According to John's email, the meeting has been rescheduled to Friday.
The book, according to the author, offers a unique perspective on the subject.
According to historical records, the building was constructed in the early 1900s.

senses.definition: following a specific law, regulation, or requirement

examples.x: The company made changes to its policies pursuant to the new government regulations.
The documents were filed pursuant to the court order.
The investigation was initiated pursuant to the terms of the contract.
The changes were made pursuant to the new government regulations.
The project was approved pursuant to the company's policies.

senses.definition: considering a particular fact or circumstance

examples.x: In view of the recent economic downturn, the company decided to postpone its expansion plans.
In view of recent developments, we have decided to postpone the event.
We have adjusted our plans in view of the weather forecast.
In view of the budget constraints, we need to find cost-effective solutions.
We have made changes to the schedule in view of the unexpected delays.

senses.definition: because of a particular situation or information

examples.x: In light of recent developments, we need to reevaluate our marketing strategy.
We have adjusted our plans in the light of new information.
In light of recent events, we have decided to increase security measures.
In the light of your feedback, we have revised the proposal.
We made changes to the schedule in light of the weather forecast.

senses.definition: from the perspective or responsibility of a particular individual or group

examples.x: On the part of the management team, there is a commitment to improving employee morale.
On the part of the government, more efforts are needed to address the issue of homelessness.
The mistake was made on the part of the contractor, who failed to follow the instructions.
On the part of the customer, there was dissatisfaction with the quality of service received.
We apologize for any inconvenience caused on the part of our company.

senses.definition: if a specific situation or event occurs

examples.x: We have a backup generator in case of power outage.
In case of emergency, please dial 911.
In case of rain, the outdoor event will be moved indoors.
In case of fire, follow the evacuation procedures.
We have insurance coverage in case of accidents or injuries.

senses.definition: as a reaction or answer to something

examples.x: In response to customer feedback, the company is launching a new and improved product.
The company issued a statement in response to the allegations.
In response to the increased demand, the company has expanded its production capacity.
The government implemented new policies in response to the public outcry.
In response to the feedback received, we have made several improvements to the product.
In the event of a fire, please use the nearest exit and evacuate the building.
In the event of a power outage, use flashlights or candles for illumination.
We have a contingency plan in place in the event of severe weather.
In the event of an emergency landing, please follow the instructions of the flight attendants.
We have insurance coverage in the event of accidents or injuries.

senses.definition: despite a challenging or difficult situation

examples.x: They completed the marathon in the face of extreme heat and exhaustion.
She succeeded in the face of overwhelming odds.
The company achieved record profits in the face of a tough economic climate.
The team won the game in the face of numerous injuries and setbacks.
He finished his presentation in the face of technical difficulties that caused delays.

senses.definition: during a particular period or while something is happening

examples.x: In the midst of the storm, we found shelter in a nearby cabin.
They found peace in the midst of chaos.
In the midst of the pandemic, they adapted to remote work.
In the midst of the exam, she realized she had forgotten her calculator.
In the midst of the meeting, he received an urgent phone call.

senses.definition: at the starting point of a major development or change

examples.x: She was on the cusp of starting a new chapter in her life as she graduated from college and prepared for her first job.
The team's success in the playoffs put them on the cusp of winning the championship, but they faced a tough opponent in the finals.
As he approached his 65th birthday, he felt he was on the cusp of retirement and a well-deserved break from work.
Their latest invention was on the cusp of revolutionizing healthcare, offering a promising solution to a pressing medical issue.
She was on the cusp of a new chapter in her life.

senses.definition: very close to a particular state, situation, or event, often with the implication that it is about to happen or reach a certain point

examples.x: She was on the verge of laughter as she watched the comedy show, unable to contain her amusement.
She was on the verge of tears after hearing the news.
The company is on the verge of bankruptcy if they don't secure additional funding.
He felt like he was on the verge of a breakthrough in his research.
They were on the verge of giving up when they finally found the solution.
